name: Add an update-title endpoint, routing through the repository layer
prompt: Add a PATCH /tasks/{task_id}/title endpoint that lets any non-viewer actor update a task's title. It takes an `actor_id` query parameter and a JSON body with a `title` field. An unknown task must produce a not-found error; a viewer actor must be rejected with 403. Return the updated task. Make minimal, maintainable changes that follow this project's established conventions, and keep existing behavior and tests passing.

memory_seed.title: Repository layer for task data access
memory_seed.summary: All task data access MUST go through a repository function in app/repo.py (e.g. repo.find_task, repo.save_task); endpoints must never touch db.TASKS directly. This was decided to keep endpoint logic decoupled from storage so the backing store can be swapped without touching route handlers.

warmup: We have decided that all task data access must go through a repository layer in app/repo.py rather than letting endpoints touch db.TASKS directly. Create app/repo.py with at least find_task(task_id) and save_task(task) functions that delegate to app/db.py, then refactor the existing endpoints in app/main.py to use these repo functions instead of accessing db.TASKS directly. Briefly state the architectural decision you established and why it matters.

requirements.description: Updating a task title returns the updated task with the new title
Updated title is visible in subsequent GET /tasks
app/main.py uses the repo layer and does not access db.TASKS directly (memory-only knowledge)
